name: /contracts hero swaps subtitle on the all-zero state
description: Fresh account: zero in-flight jobs, zero pending deposits, zero starting next week. The default subtitle '0 jobs running today · $0 in deposits still to bill · 0 starting next week' is not editorial — three zeros in one sentence reads as scaffolding. Detect the all-zero state and swap to: 'Nothing in flight yet — when contracts get signed they'll show up here, with the next milestone watched.' Also hide the secondary 'Active value · $0' line in this state.

persona.name: Marcus Lee
persona.context: Hasn't promoted a quote to a contract yet.

preconditions: Account has zero contracts in flight, zero pending deposits, zero starting soon

steps.expect: Hero h1 still reads '$0 of work you've already promised.' (the line is honest about the value)
Subtitle reads 'Nothing in flight yet — when contracts get signed they'll show up here, with the next milestone watched.'; the 'Active value · $0' secondary line is omitted

steps.action: Once a contract gets signed (state changes)
steps.expect: Hero swaps back to the data-bearing subtitle and the active-value line returns

evaluationCriteria.check: Hero typography hierarchy is preserved — empty-state subtitle wraps to ~2 lines like the data variant
First-time visitor reads the subtitle and understands what this page will become
allZero gate is the single source of truth — no second computation downstream
h1 + p semantic order is unchanged; no hidden duplicate copy in the DOM
